Proof 
                         Suppose the hypothesis holds. In particular, we have  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  a 
                                  − 
                                  1 
                                  ) 
                               
                               + 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  b 
                                  − 
                                  1 
                                  ) 
                               
                               = 
                               8 
                            . Notice that no matter how we take two nonnegative numbers that add up to  
                               8 
                            , one of the numbers must be in  
                               E 
                            . So either  
                               a 
                               − 
                               1 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                             or  
                               b 
                               − 
                               1 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                             holds. Assume without loss of generality that  
                               a 
                               − 
                               1 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                            . Observe that  
                               E 
                             is closed under addition, except for the fact that  
                               4 
                               + 
                               4 
                               = 
                               8 
                             with  
                               4 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                             but  
                               8 
                               ∉ 
                               E 
                            . Since  
                               a 
                               − 
                               1 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                             and  
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               − 
                               1 
                               = 
                               a 
                               − 
                               1 
                               + 
                               Σ 
                               
                                  
                                     c 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                             with each  
                               
                                  
                                     c 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                            , it follows that  
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               − 
                               1 
                             is a sum of numbers of  
                               E 
                            . The closure property of  
                               E 
                             implies that  
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               − 
                               1 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                             (in which case we are done), unless: 
                         
                            
                               
                                  (1) 
                                  either  
                                        a 
                                        − 
                                        1 
                                        = 
                                        4 
                                     , and in the summation there is exactly one positive  
                                        
                                           
                                              c 
                                           
                                           
                                              i 
                                           
                                        
                                     , equal to  
                                        4 
                                     ; 
                               
                               
                                  (2) 
                                  or  
                                        a 
                                        − 
                                        1 
                                        = 
                                        0 
                                     , and in the summation there are exactly two positive  
                                        
                                           
                                              c 
                                           
                                           
                                              i 
                                           
                                        
                                     , both equal to  
                                        4 
                                     . 
                               
                            
                         
                         In both cases, we have  
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               9 
                            . Since  
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               + 
                               
                                  
                                     b 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               ≤ 
                               15 
                            , it follows that  
                               
                                  
                                     b 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               ≤ 
                               6 
                            . In case (2), we have  
                               a 
                               = 
                               1 
                            , hence  
                               b 
                               = 
                               9 
                            , contradicting the hypothesis  
                               
                                  
                                     b 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               ≥ 
                               b 
                            . Thus, case (1) holds, which implies  
                               a 
                               = 
                               b 
                               = 
                               5 
                            . Since  
                               
                                  
                                     b 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               b 
                               + 
                               Σ 
                               
                                  
                                     d 
                                  
                                  
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               5 
                               + 
                               Σ 
                               
                                  
                                     d 
                                  
                                  
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                               ≤ 
                               6 
                             with each  
                               
                                  
                                     d 
                                  
                                  
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                            , we must have  
                               Σ 
                               
                                  
                                     d 
                                  
                                  
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                               ≤ 
                               1 
                            . The definition of  
                               E 
                             yields  
                               Σ 
                               
                                  
                                     d 
                                  
                                  
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               0 
                            , hence  
                               
                                  
                                     b 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               b 
                               = 
                               5 
                            . So  
                               
                                  
                                     b 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               − 
                               1 
                               = 
                               4 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                            , and we are done.  □

Suppose the hypothesis holds. In particular, we have  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  a 
                                  − 
                                  1 
                                  ) 
                               
                               + 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  b 
                                  − 
                                  1 
                                  ) 
                               
                               = 
                               8 
                            . Notice that no matter how we take two nonnegative numbers that add up to  
                               8 
                            , one of the numbers must be in  
                               E 
                            . So either  
                               a 
                               − 
                               1 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                             or  
                               b 
                               − 
                               1 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                             holds. Assume without loss of generality that  
                               a 
                               − 
                               1 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                            . Observe that  
                               E 
                             is closed under addition, except for the fact that  
                               4 
                               + 
                               4 
                               = 
                               8 
                             with  
                               4 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                             but  
                               8 
                               ∉ 
                               E 
                            . Since  
                               a 
                               − 
                               1 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                             and  
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               − 
                               1 
                               = 
                               a 
                               − 
                               1 
                               + 
                               Σ 
                               
                                  
                                     c 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                             with each  
                               
                                  
                                     c 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                            , it follows that  
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               − 
                               1 
                             is a sum of numbers of  
                               E 
                            . The closure property of  
                               E 
                             implies that  
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               − 
                               1 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                             (in which case we are done), unless:

(1) 
                                  either  
                                        a 
                                        − 
                                        1 
                                        = 
                                        4 
                                     , and in the summation there is exactly one positive  
                                        
                                           
                                              c 
                                           
                                           
                                              i 
                                           
                                        
                                     , equal to  
                                        4 
                                     ; 
                               
                               
                                  (2) 
                                  or  
                                        a 
                                        − 
                                        1 
                                        = 
                                        0 
                                     , and in the summation there are exactly two positive  
                                        
                                           
                                              c 
                                           
                                           
                                              i 
                                           
                                        
                                     , both equal to  
                                        4 
                                     .

either  
                                        a 
                                        − 
                                        1 
                                        = 
                                        4 
                                     , and in the summation there is exactly one positive  
                                        
                                           
                                              c 
                                           
                                           
                                              i 
                                           
                                        
                                     , equal to  
                                        4

or

                                              i 
                                           
                                        
                                     , both equal to  
                                        4

In both cases, we have

                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               9 
                            . Since

                                     b 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               ≤ 
                               15

                                     b 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               ≤ 
                               6

                               a

                               b

                               9

                               ≥ 
                               b

                               a 
                               = 
                               b 
                               = 
                               5 
                            . Since  
                               
                                  
                                     b 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′

                               b 
                               + 
                               Σ 
                               
                                  
                                     d 
                                  
                                  
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               5 
                               + 
                               Σ 
                               
                                  
                                     d 
                                  
                                  
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                               ≤ 
                               6 
                             with each  
                               
                                  
                                     d 
                                  
                                  
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                            , we must have  
                               Σ 
                               
                                  
                                     d 
                                  
                                  
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                               ≤ 
                               1 
                            . The definition of  
                               E 
                             yields  
                               Σ 
                               
                                  
                                     d 
                                  
                                  
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               0 
                            , hence

                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               =

                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               − 
                               1 
                               = 
                               4 
                               ∈ 
                               E 
                            , and we are done.  □

Example 9.3  is an instance of the following fact:

Lemma 9.4

The next definition introduces a family of subsets of  
                         N

                               k

Proof 
                         Assume that  
                               n 
                               , 
                               k 
                               ∈ 
                               N 
                             are such that  
                               n 
                               ≥ 
                               k 
                               + 
                               4 
                            ,  
                               k 
                               ≥ 
                               1 
                             and  
                               n 
                               ≢ 
                               3 
                               
                               
                                  ( 
                                  mod 
                                  
                                  k 
                                  ) 
                               
                            . 
                         
                            
                               
                                  (i) 
                                  Obviously,  
                                        0 
                                        ∈ 
                                        E 
                                      and  
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                        ∉ 
                                        E 
                                     . To show that  
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                        − 
                                        k 
                                        ∉ 
                                        E 
                                     , we consider two cases: (A)  
                                        0 
                                        ≤ 
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                        − 
                                        k 
                                        < 
                                        
                                           
                                              n 
                                              − 
                                              3 
                                           
                                           
                                              2 
                                           
                                        
                                     ; (B)  
                                        
                                           
                                              n 
                                              − 
                                              3 
                                           
                                           
                                              2 
                                           
                                        
                                        ≤ 
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                        − 
                                        k 
                                        < 
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        2 
                                     . In case (A), if  
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                        − 
                                        k 
                                        ≡ 
                                        0 
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           mod 
                                           
                                           k 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      were to hold, then we would have  
                                        n 
                                        ≡ 
                                        3 
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           mod 
                                           
                                           k 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     , a contradiction. It follows that  
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                        − 
                                        k 
                                        ∉ 
                                        E 
                                     . In case (B), we have  
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                        − 
                                        k 
                                        ≡ 
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           mod 
                                           
                                           k 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     , and again  
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                        − 
                                        k 
                                        ∉ 
                                        E 
                                     . 
                               
                               
                                  (ii) 
                                  By definition of  
                                        E 
                                        = 
                                        E 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           n 
                                           , 
                                           k 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     . 
                               
                               
                                  (iii) 
                                  Suppose  
                                        ∑ 
                                        
                                           { 
                                           
                                              
                                                 b 
                                              
                                              
                                                 i 
                                              
                                           
                                           : 
                                           i 
                                           ∈ 
                                           P 
                                           } 
                                        
                                        ∉ 
                                        E 
                                      with each  
                                        
                                           
                                              b 
                                           
                                           
                                              i 
                                           
                                        
                                        ∈ 
                                        E 
                                     . Let  
                                        Q 
                                        ≔ 
                                        
                                           { 
                                           
                                              i 
                                              ∈ 
                                              P 
                                              : 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    b 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    i 
                                                 
                                              
                                              ≥

                                                    − 
                                                    3 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    2 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                           } 
                                        
                                     . In what follows, we show that

                                           Q 
                                           | 
                                        
                                        = 
                                        2 
                                     ,

                                              i 
                                           
                                        
                                        = 
                                        0 
                                      for each  
                                        i 
                                        ∈

                                        ∖ 
                                        Q 
                                     : this will prove the claim. To start, observe that the hypothesis implies that  
                                        
                                           
                                              b

                                        0 
                                      for each  
                                        i

                                           ∈ 
                                           Q 
                                           }

                                        ∑

                                           : 
                                           i 
                                           ∈ 
                                           P 
                                           } 
                                        
                                     . Further, since  
                                        n 
                                        ≢

                                           (4)

                                                    x 
                                                    ∉ 
                                                    E

                                                    x 
                                                    >

                                                    ≡ 
                                                    x

                                              ∉ 
                                              E 
                                           
                                        
                                     holds for each  
                                        x 
                                        , 
                                        y 
                                        ∈ 
                                        N 
                                     . Thus  (4)  readily yields  
                                        ∑ 
                                        
                                           { 
                                           
                                              
                                                 b 
                                              
                                              
                                                 i 
                                              
                                           
                                           : 
                                           i 
                                           ∈ 
                                           Q 
                                           } 
                                        
                                        ∉ 
                                        E 
                                     . In particular,  
                                        0 
                                        < 
                                        ∑ 
                                        
                                           {

                                                 i 
                                              
                                           
                                           : 
                                           i 
                                           ∈ 
                                           Q 
                                           }

                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                      by the definition of  
                                        E 
                                        = 
                                        E 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           n 
                                           , 
                                           k 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     . Since

                                              −

                                      for  
                                        i 
                                        ∈ 
                                        Q 
                                     . Since  
                                        ∑ 
                                        
                                           { 
                                           
                                              
                                                 b 
                                              
                                              
                                                 i 
                                              
                                           
                                           : 
                                           i 
                                           ∈ 
                                           P 
                                           } 
                                        
                                        ≤ 
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3

                                        = 
                                        0 
                                      for  
                                        i 
                                        ∈

                                        ∖

                                     , as required. 
                               
                               
                                  (iv) 
                                  Suppose  
                                        a 
                                        ,

                                        ≥ 
                                        0

                                        a

                                        a

                                        a 
                                        < 
                                        
                                           
                                              n 
                                              − 
                                              3

                                        <

                                     , then  
                                        a 
                                        ≢ 
                                        0 
                                     , and  
                                        b 
                                        ≡ 
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                     , so  
                                        a 
                                        + 
                                        b 
                                        ≢ 
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                     , which is false. If  
                                        a 
                                        = 
                                        b 
                                        = 
                                        
                                           
                                              n 
                                              − 
                                              3 
                                           
                                           
                                              2 
                                           
                                        
                                     , then  
                                        
                                           
                                              n 
                                              − 
                                              3 
                                           
                                           
                                              2 
                                           
                                        
                                        ≢ 
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                      implies  
                                        a 
                                        , 
                                        b 
                                        ∈ 
                                        E 
                                     .  □

Assume that  
                               n 
                               , 
                               k 
                               ∈ 
                               N 
                             are such that  
                               n 
                               ≥ 
                               k 
                               + 
                               4 
                            ,  
                               k 
                               ≥ 
                               1 
                             and  
                               n 
                               ≢ 
                               3 
                               
                               
                                  ( 
                                  mod 
                                  
                                  k 
                                  ) 
                               
                            .

(i) 
                                  Obviously,  
                                        0 
                                        ∈ 
                                        E 
                                      and  
                                        n 
                                        − 
                                        3 
                                        ∉ 
                                        E 
                                     . To show that  
                                        n 
                                        −

Now the necessity of  Theorem 6.1  readily follows from  Lemma 5.3  (i) and  Corollary 9.12 . This completes the proof of the main result of this paper.  □

In this paper we have studied strict  
                         
                            ( 
                            m 
                            , 
                            1 
                            ) 
                         
                      -Ferrers properties, which are axioms connected to degrees of transitivity of a strict preference relation: loosely speaking, the larger  
                         m 
                      , the weaker the property. The relevance of these properties in applications to economics/psychology lies in the fact that they are connected to money-pump phenomena. The main result of this paper completely describes the poset of strict  
                         
                            ( 
                            m 
                            , 
                            1 
                            ) 
                         
                      -Ferrers properties.

Further research on the topic is to be devoted to the study of the behavior of all  strict  
                      
                         
                            ( 
                            m 
                            , 
                            n 
                            ) 
                         
                      -Ferrers properties. In fact, one could focus on all categories of binary relations that do not exhibit certain mixed cycles of strict preferences and indifferences, since these cycles may typically generate unwanted phenomena in applications. This naturally leads to the notion of a  generalized cycle , whose avoidance gives new forms of pseudo-transitivity: weak and strict Ferrers properties are just very special cases of this kind.

The authors thank Salvatore Corrente for some computations aimed at testing their conjectures. They are also grateful to the managing editor Jean-Paul Doignon and three anonymous referees for their helpful comments and suggestions. The first author acknowledges partial funding by “ Università di Catania 
                   FIR-2014 BCAEA3 ”.
